raw:
- The utility buttons, bug report, stats, and help need to be attached to the top bar near the user icon rather than in the pane.

- make the settings menu comprehensive. I should be able to navigate server settings and regular settings in one interface with clear distinctions.

- make sure that when I send a message my pane is properly scrolled to the bottom of the chat display.

- we need to get the native builds ready to build and test. build a native arch application for the concorrd frontened

- I need the native application goal to come sooner rather than later. Do everything you can to do the work on orrion and anything that needs to be done on orrpheus for apple reasons should be delegated there.

- the dashboard bar at the bottom of the small display (mobile) takes up space that the user needs. collapse that entire rown to a handful of cucumber shapes so the user can navigate between if they know the labels, or they can close their keyboard/click on the buttons in otder to see the dash

   - the bar for bug reports, stats and help also takes up valuable real estate

   - both of these need to move or be collapsed when in the focused view on mobile

- the keyboard should be flagged to use the return button to move to the next line rather than send the message. The user presses the send button to send. The return button goes to the next line

- add make it easy for openclaw to create and present visual presentations like charts
optimized:
### OPT-001: Move utility buttons to top bar
Relocate bug report, stats, and help buttons from the main pane into the top bar adjacent to the user icon. Remove them from their current in-pane location.

Source: Bullet 1 (utility buttons attached to top bar)

### OPT-002: Unified comprehensive settings interface
Build a single settings menu that exposes both server settings and user/regular settings in one navigable interface. Use clear visual separation (sections, headers, or tabs) to distinguish server-scope from user-scope settings.

Source: Bullet 2 (comprehensive settings menu)

### OPT-003: Auto-scroll chat pane on message send
Ensure the chat display auto-scrolls to the bottom of the message list whenever the local user sends a message. Verify behavior across room switches and long histories.

Source: Bullet 3 (pane scrolled to bottom on send)

### OPT-004: Native frontend build pipeline
Set up the build toolchain to produce a native architecture application from the Concord frontend. Deliver a working build+test pipeline for native desktop targets. Prioritize Linux (orrion) first; document prerequisites.

Source: Bullet 4 (native builds ready to build and test)

### OPT-005: Accelerate native app delivery, split platform work across machines
Treat native application delivery as a high-priority milestone. Perform all non-Apple platform work on orrion. Delegate any Apple-platform-specific build/signing/notarization work to orrpheus (macOS M1 Pro). Coordinate handoffs between the two machines.

Source: Bullet 5 (native app sooner; orrion + orrpheus split)

### OPT-006: Collapse mobile dashboard bar into compact pills
On mobile/small displays, collapse the bottom dashboard bar into a small row of pill ("cucumber") shaped buttons. Users familiar with labels can navigate directly; otherwise they dismiss the keyboard or tap a pill to expand the full dashboard.

Source: Bullet 6 (mobile dashboard bar collapse)

### OPT-007: Collapse mobile utility bar in focused view
On mobile focused/chat view, collapse or hide the bug report / stats / help bar so it does not consume vertical space. Combine with OPT-001 (top-bar relocation) where possible.

Source: Bullet 6 sub-bullets (utility bar collapse on mobile)

### OPT-008: Mobile keyboard Return key inserts newline
Configure the mobile message input so the keyboard Return key inserts a newline instead of sending. Sending is triggered only by the explicit Send button. Apply on iOS and Android native builds and mobile web.

Source: Bullet 7 (return = newline, send button = send)

### OPT-009: Visual presentation authoring for OpenClaw
Add affordances that let OpenClaw easily create and present visual content (charts, graphs, simple presentations) inline in chat. Support at minimum bar/line/pie charts; render via a lightweight chart library and persist as message attachments.

Source: Bullet 8 (visual presentations and charts for openclaw)

## Status inquiry (not routed — answered directly)

Yes — per OPT-004 and OPT-005, the Concord plan is a native application on all supported platforms (Linux/Windows via orrion, macOS/iOS via orrpheus), with native delivery now prioritized as a near-term milestone.
